meta.title: BetterDay Platform — Project Scope
meta.subtitle: By-category module tree for the D2C storefront, subscription engine, and admin console

1.label: P1 — Foundation
1.description: Immediate. Must exist before any other work happens.

2.label: P2 — Core
2.description: Next layer. Built on P1, required for a functional platform.

4.label: P4 — Post-launch soon
4.description: Not needed day one but wanted within a month.

5.label: P5 — Future
5.description: Out of scope for 2026. Explore later.

done.label: Done
done.description: Built, tested, and live (or ready to be).

designed.label: Designed
designed.description: Schema / demo / prior design exists, ready to implement.

to-build.label: To build
to-build.description: Identified and in scope, not started.

blocked.label: Blocked
blocked.description: Cannot proceed until a dependency or decision resolves.

skipped.label: Skipped
skipped.description: Consciously dropped from scope. Kept visible for context.

categories.name: Marketing & Public Site
categories.description: Unauthenticated public pages: homepage, menu teaser, story, FAQ, legal, SEO. The face of BetterDay.
categories.tech_notes: Next.js 14 App Router, SSG/SSR hybrid. Port from existing betterday-v2_81.html. Shared brand system.

modules.name: Public Pages
modules.description: Every statically-renderable page customers can land on without logging in.

components.name: Hero section
components.description: Above-the-fold: headline, sub, primary CTA, hero image, price anchor pill, location pill.

tech.notes: Port from betterday-v2_81.html. Mobile-first.

components.name: How It Works
components.description: Three-step explainer. Cards with icons, subtle brand colour blocks.

tech.notes: Ported from homepage draft.

components.name: Menu teaser
components.description: Shows 6-8 meals from current week with 'See full menu' CTA.

tech.notes: Pulls live menu via API.

components.name: Why BetterDay race
components.description: Interactive race animation comparing BetterDay vs takeout vs groceries.

tech.notes: IntersectionObserver auto-start. Canvas or CSS animation.

components.name: Order Types card
components.description: Side-by-side subscribe vs one-time cards with price pills.

components.name: Savings slider
components.description: Interactive slider showing savings as cart grows.

tech.notes: Auto-animates on scroll, confetti at free-delivery threshold.

components.name: Social proof section
components.description: Calgary testimonials carousel.

components.name: Our Story (Amy & Conner)
components.description: Founder story section with photo. Positioned before final CTA.

tech.notes: Copy still being drafted.

components.name: Final CTA
components.description: Bottom-of-page conversion card with risk-reversal copy.

components.name: Public menu grid
components.description: Non-logged-in view of current week's menu with filters. Clicking a meal shows detail.

tech.notes: Port from menu-overlay.html.

components.name: Meal detail modal
components.description: Side-by-side 55/45 layout: photo + name + macros + allergens.

components.name: Savings tier calculator
components.description: Standalone calculator showing savings at each cart size.

components.name: Plan comparison card
components.description: One-time vs subscription at various cart sizes.

components.name: Founders bio
components.description: Amy & Conner bio with photos.

components.name: Mission + values
components.description: Brand values block. Compostable, local, etc.

components.name: FAQ categories
components.description: Accordion grouped by topic (ordering, delivery, subscriptions, dietary, payments).

tech.notes: Content in MDX or a simple JSON source.

components.name: FAQ search
components.description: Client-side fuzzy search over FAQ content.

tech.notes: fuse.js or similar.

components.name: Terms of Service
components.description: Required for launch. Lawyer review needed.

components.name: Privacy Policy
components.description: Required for launch. PIPEDA compliant.

components.name: Accessibility statement
components.description: AODA statement for Alberta.

components.name: Refund policy
components.description: Customer-facing refund rules. Linked from checkout + footer.

components.name: Contact form
components.description: Simple name/email/message form, routes to support inbox.

components.name: Hours + location
components.description: Kitchen location, delivery zones, customer support hours.

components.name: Blog index
components.description: List of posts. MDX or CMS-backed.

components.name: Blog post template
components.description: Single post template with related posts.

modules.name: SEO & Discovery
modules.description: Making sure Google and social platforms understand and surface the site.

components.name: Open Graph tags
components.description: Per-page og:title, og:description, og:image.

tech.notes: Next.js metadata API.

components.name: Twitter card tags
components.description: summary_large_image cards per page.

components.name: Canonical URLs
components.description: Prevent duplicate content indexing.

components.name: Organization schema
components.description: BetterDay org schema with logo, social, address.

components.name: Menu item schema
components.description: Product schema for each meal (name, image, offers).

components.name: LocalBusiness schema
components.description: Kitchen address, opening hours, delivery area.

components.name: sitemap.xml
components.description: Auto-generated from Next.js routes.

components.name: robots.txt
components.description: Allow all except /admin and /api.

components.name: Google Analytics 4
components.description: Page views, custom events, conversion tracking.

components.name: Meta Pixel
components.description: Facebook/Instagram conversion tracking.

components.name: Cookie consent banner
components.description: GDPR/PIPEDA-style consent before firing trackers.

tech.notes: cookiebot or self-hosted.

modules.name: Brand System
modules.description: Design tokens and shared visual assets used across every surface.

components.name: Colour tokens
components.description: Cream #FAEBDA, sky #4EA2FD, navy #003141, yellow #FFC600, purple #C8A4F5. Plus greens.

tech.notes: Locked; documented in design-tokens.md.

components.name: Typography tokens
components.description: BDSupper (meal names), Gaya (section headers), Sofia Pro (body/UI), Inter (system).

tech.notes: self-hosted WOFF2, preload critical subsets.

components.name: Radius + shadow tokens
components.description: 20px card radius, 100px pill radius, 7px 7px hover shadow.

components.name: Spacing scale
components.description: Standard Tailwind spacing with brand-specific overrides.

components.name: Button component
components.description: Primary (yellow), secondary (outline), ghost variants. Pill shape.

tech.notes: shadcn/ui as base, themed to brand.

components.name: Pill component
components.description: Section pill labels, status pills, price pills. Consistent sizing.

components.name: Card component
components.description: Standard card with shadow variants.

components.name: Nav bar
components.description: Sticky sky-blue header with logo + nav links + cart icon + login/account.

components.name: Footer
components.description: Navy footer with navigation, subscribe form, social links, contact.

components.name: Logo files
components.description: SVG + PNG at multiple sizes, light + dark variants.

tech.notes: Store in /public/brand/ or CDN.

components.name: Favicons + app icons
components.description: ICO, PNG, Apple touch icons, Android icons, manifest.json.

components.name: Social share images
components.description: OG images per key page (1200x630).

tech.notes: Next.js opengraph-image.tsx or pre-rendered.

categories.name: Identity & Auth
categories.description: Who is logging in, how they prove it, and the sessions that keep them logged in. The gatekeeper for everything downstream.
categories.tech_notes: Own our own auth. NestJS module in culinary-ops. Postgres (Prisma) for AuthIdentity/Session/OtpCode/OAuthLink. Twilio Verify for SMS. Resend for email. Google + Facebook OAuth with our own app registrations. JWT access tokens (15 min) + opaque refresh tokens (30 days) in httpOnly cookies. argon2id for password hashing.

modules.name: Auth Foundation
modules.description: Schemas, middleware, and primitives every other auth module depends on.

components.name: AuthIdentity model
components.description: Primary auth row. One per phone or email. Holds password hash, verification timestamps, last login.

tech.notes: Fields: id, email?, phone?, password_hash?, email_verified_at?, phone_verified_at?, created_at, last_login_at

components.name: Session model
components.description: Refresh-token sessions. One row per active login.

tech.notes: Fields: id, identity_id, refresh_token_hash, user_agent, ip_address, expires_at, revoked_at?

components.name: OtpCode model
components.description: One-time codes for SMS or email OTP. Short TTL, hashed values, attempt counter.

tech.notes: Fields: id, identity_id?, destination, channel (sms|email), code_hash, attempts, expires_at, consumed_at?

components.name: OAuthLink model
components.description: Links third-party identity providers (Google, Facebook) to an AuthIdentity.

tech.notes: Fields: id, identity_id, provider, provider_id, email, linked_at. @@unique([provider, provider_id])

components.name: JWT signer + verifier
components.description: Sign short-lived access tokens, verify on every protected request. Refresh flow.

tech.notes: jsonwebtoken or jose. 15-minute access token, 30-day refresh. Our own signing secret from env.

components.name: AuthGuard decorator
components.description: Decorator to protect API routes. Extracts identity from JWT, attaches to request context.

tech.notes: @UseGuards(AuthGuard) on protected controllers.

components.name: Rate limiter
components.description: Per-IP and per-identity rate limits on login, signup, OTP send, password reset.

tech.notes: @nestjs/throttler or a Redis-backed custom limiter.

modules.name: Phone OTP Login
modules.description: Passwordless login with a 6-digit SMS code. The default sign-in method for most customers.

components.name: Send OTP endpoint
components.description: POST /api/auth/phone/start — normalizes phone, looks up or creates AuthIdentity, triggers Twilio Verify send, returns otp_id.

tech.notes: Twilio Verify SID in env. Uses their service — we don't manage code generation or storage ourselves.

components.name: Verify OTP endpoint
components.description: POST /api/auth/phone/verify — checks code with Twilio, marks phone verified, creates session, returns JWT + refresh token.

components.name: Phone input + country code
components.description: Phone number input with masked formatting and country selector. Defaults to Canada.

tech.notes: libphonenumber-js for validation and formatting.

components.name: 6-digit code entry
components.description: Auto-advancing 6-box code entry with paste support and resend link.

tech.notes: Resend button disabled for 30s after a send.

modules.name: Email + Password Login
modules.description: Traditional email/password signup and login. Required for customers who don't want to share a phone number.

components.name: POST /api/auth/email/signup
components.description: Create AuthIdentity with argon2id password hash, send verification email, create session.

tech.notes: argon2id with sensible work factors. Verification email sent via Resend.

components.name: Email verification link
components.description: One-time link that marks email_verified_at. Expires in 24h.

components.name: POST /api/auth/login
components.description: Verify password, create session, return tokens. Handles 'must_reset_password' case for HMP legacy migrants.

tech.notes: Returns 401 with reason='must_reset_password' when legacy HMP user has no local hash.

components.name: Legacy detection
components.description: On login attempt for unknown email, check HMP /v1/tenant/customers. If found, trigger password reset email instead of 401.

tech.notes: Uses HMP customer lookup. Marks AuthIdentity with legacy flag once created.

components.name: 'Set new password' email
components.description: One-time email explaining the platform upgrade and linking to password reset.

tech.notes: Custom template separate from normal password reset.

modules.name: OAuth (Google, Facebook)
modules.description: Sign in with Google or Facebook. Uses our own registered OAuth apps so the consent screen shows 'BetterDay', not 'Happy Meal Prep'.

components.name: Register Google OAuth app
components.description: Create BetterDay OAuth client in Google Cloud Console. Redirect URI → /api/auth/oauth/google/callback.

tech.notes: One-time setup. Consent screen branding review if we need production access.

components.name: Callback endpoint
components.description: Exchange code → token → profile. Upsert OAuthLink, AuthIdentity. Create session.

components.name: Register Facebook app
components.description: Facebook for Developers → new app. Review required for production email scope.

tech.notes: App review is slower than Google. Start early.

components.name: Callback endpoint
components.description: Same shape as Google callback.

modules.name: Session Management
modules.description: Active-session lifecycle, refresh token rotation, logout, multi-device support.

components.name: POST /api/auth/refresh
components.description: Trade opaque refresh token for new access token. Rotate refresh token on every use.

tech.notes: Refresh stored as httpOnly cookie. Rotation detects replay — if old token used after rotation, revoke entire session chain.

components.name: Logout current session
components.description: Revoke the current refresh token, clear cookie.

components.name: Logout all devices
components.description: Revoke every session for this identity. Used after password change or suspected compromise.

components.name: List + revoke sessions UI
components.description: Account page showing all active sessions with user agent, IP, last active. Per-session revoke button.

modules.name: Account Recovery
modules.description: Forgot password, password reset, lost-phone recovery.

components.name: POST /api/auth/forgot-password
components.description: Accept email, send reset link via Resend. Always respond 200 even if email doesn't exist (no user enumeration).

components.name: POST /api/auth/reset-password
components.description: Accept token + new password, verify, update hash, revoke all existing sessions.

categories.name: Customer Profile
categories.description: Profile info, preferences, dietary restrictions, saved payment methods, communication settings.
categories.tech_notes: NestJS module + Next.js account pages. Talks to Customer, CustomerAddress, AuthIdentity tables.

modules.name: Profile Data Model
modules.description: Customer profile table separated from AuthIdentity so OAuth-only users still have a profile.

components.name: Customer Prisma model
components.description: One-to-one with AuthIdentity. Fields: id, identity_id, first_name, last_name, display_name, marketing_opt_in, lifecycle_stage, hmp_customer_id, created_at, notes.

tech.notes: hmp_customer_id exists only for migration traceability.

components.name: Lifecycle stage enum
components.description: prospect | active | paused | churned. Drives re-engagement campaigns and admin filters.

tech.notes: Enum in Prisma schema. Updated by subscription state transitions.

components.name: CustomerAddress Prisma model
components.description: Fields: id, customer_id, label, line1, line2, city, province, postal_code, country, delivery_notes, is_default_delivery, is_default_billing, created_at, archived_at.

tech.notes: Soft delete via archived_at so old orders still resolve.

components.name: CustomerPreference model
components.description: Dietary restrictions, allergens, portion size, flavour dislikes. Feeds menu filtering and auto-swap rules.

modules.name: Profile API
modules.description: Read/write endpoints for profile data, called from the account hub.

components.name: GET /api/account/me
components.description: Returns current customer + addresses + preferences + lifecycle summary.

components.name: PUT /api/account/profile
components.description: Update name, display name, phone, marketing opt-in.

components.name: Address CRUD
components.description: POST/PUT/DELETE /api/account/addresses. Sets default_delivery / default_billing flags atomically.

components.name: PUT /api/account/preferences
components.description: Update dietary restrictions, allergens, portion size.

components.name: Email change with verification
components.description: POST triggers a verification email; new email takes effect only after link clicked.

components.name: Phone change with OTP
components.description: POST triggers an SMS verification to the new number; only updates on verify.

modules.name: Profile UI
modules.description: Account hub pages where customers manage their info.

components.name: Account layout shell
components.description: Navy nav bar, tabs for My Subscription / Customer Info / Billing / Order History / Discounts, mobile-responsive.

tech.notes: Port from subscriber-hub.html ECC-style nav.

components.name: Profile edit form
components.description: Name, display name, phone, email, marketing opt-in toggle.

components.name: Password + security section
components.description: Change password, enabled 2FA methods, manage sessions.

components.name: Dietary preferences form
components.description: Checkbox list: vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, nut-free, dairy-free, etc.

components.name: Address list with default badge
components.description: Cards for each address with 'Set default delivery' / 'Edit' / 'Delete' actions.

components.name: Add/edit address form
components.description: Autocomplete postal code, validate against delivery zones.

tech.notes: Google Places or manual entry fallback.

components.name: Channel toggles
components.description: Email / SMS / Push toggles per notification category.

categories.name: Menu & Catalog
categories.description: Public and logged-in browsing of the weekly menu. Meal detail pages, filters, allergens, macros.
categories.tech_notes: Reads from culinary-ops MealRecipe/MealComponent tables. No duplicate data — the kitchen ops schema is the source of truth for meals.

modules.name: Menu Source of Truth
modules.description: Culinary-ops owns all meal data. The webapp reads via a thin bridge, never duplicates.

components.name: Fetch weekly menu from culinary-ops
components.description: Service that reads MealRecipe + MealComponent + PortionSpec from culinary-ops Postgres. Cached in Redis or in-memory.

tech.notes: Same Postgres DB as culinary-ops; direct query, no HTTP hop.

components.name: Meal photo URL resolution
components.description: Resolve meal_code -> photo URL via S3/R2 or local bundle.

tech.notes: Move off local disk to S3/R2 before launch. Meal photos currently in /Copy of BetterDay Meal Photos (1)/.

components.name: Weekly availability window
components.description: Reads culinary-ops CategoryAvailability + PortionSpec to determine what's on this week's menu.

tech.notes: Respects category_availability windows + holidays.

components.name: Weekly menu advance job
components.description: Cron runs every Sunday night to flip next week's menu live, triggering auto-swap on subscription carts.

modules.name: Menu API
modules.description: REST endpoints the frontend calls to render menu pages.

components.name: GET /api/menu/current
components.description: Returns the current week's menu: meals grouped by category, with photos, macros, allergens.

components.name: GET /api/menu/meal/:code
components.description: Detail for a single meal by BD-NNN code.

components.name: GET /api/menu/week/:isoWeek
components.description: Historical / future week lookup. Powers 'what's coming next week' previews.

components.name: GET /api/menu/search?q=...
components.description: Server-side search across meal name/description.

components.name: GET /api/menu/filter?diet=vegan
components.description: Filter by dietary tag or allergen.

modules.name: Browse Surface (UI)
modules.description: The visible grid where customers pick meals. Exists as demo HTML.

components.name: Meal cards (4-wide grid)
components.description: Photo, BDSupper name, category pill, macros, add-to-cart button. Hover state.

tech.notes: Port from menu-overlay.html. 4-col desktop, 2-col tablet, 1-col mobile.

components.name: Category tabs
components.description: All / Entrees / Sandwiches & Wraps / Breakfasts / Snacks & Other.

components.name: Sort & filter dropdown
components.description: Price asc/desc, macro targets, dietary tags.

components.name: Meat/vegan colour coding
components.description: Blue bg = meat, green bg = vegan for fast visual parsing.

components.name: Empty filter state
components.description: 'No meals match these filters' with reset CTA.

components.name: Allergen filter
components.description: Multi-select to exclude meals containing specific allergens.

components.name: Dietary tags filter
components.description: Vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, high-protein, etc.

components.name: Macro range filter
components.description: Min/max sliders for protein, carbs, fat, calories.

modules.name: Meal Detail
modules.description: Modal or page showing everything about one meal.

components.name: 55/45 layout
components.description: Photo on left, details on right. Name, tags, allergens, macros, description, CTA.

components.name: Macro strip
components.description: Protein / carbs / fat / calories pills.

components.name: Allergen warning pills
components.description: Highlighted pills for contains + may-contain.

components.name: Quantity control + Add/Swap
components.description: +/- qty buttons, 'Add to cart' for new items, 'Swap' for already-in-cart.

categories.name: Cart & Build-a-Cart
categories.description: The signature BetterDay feature. Dynamic cart replaces rigid plan tiers. Customer builds any cart size; savings scale with cart, not commitment length.
categories.tech_notes: Cart / CartItem tables in culinary-ops Postgres. Prior design: subscriber-hub.html, menu-overlay.html, swap panel demo. Savings tiers: 4→5%, 5→8%, 7→11%, 8→Free Delivery, 9→14%, 11→17%, 13→20%.

modules.name: Cart Data Model
modules.description: Tables and state primitives for a customer's live cart.

components.name: Cart model
components.description: One active cart per customer. Converts to an Order at checkout.

tech.notes: Fields: id, customer_id, status (active|abandoned|converted), created_at, updated_at

components.name: CartItem model
components.description: Line items referencing MealRecipe. Quantity and price snapshot.

tech.notes: FK to MealRecipe (culinary-ops existing table). unit_price_cents denormalized for stable cart math.

components.name: Frontend cart store
components.description: React state (Zustand or context) for the cart. Mirrors server state, optimistic updates on add/remove.

tech.notes: Hydrates from server on page load. Optimistic updates fall back on error.

components.name: Client ↔ server sync
components.description: Debounced PATCH to backend whenever cart changes. Resolves conflicts by last-write-wins on the server.

tech.notes: Debounce 300ms client-side.

modules.name: Cart UI
modules.description: The visible shopping surface. Exists as demo HTML — needs port to React components.

components.name: Grouped line items
components.description: Gaya-font category headers ('Entrées | 7 Total'), 46px thumbnails, stacked qty/swap actions, trash-at-qty-1.

tech.notes: Design tokens in design-tokens.md. Demo lives in subscriber-hub.html cart sidebar.

components.name: Savings tier strip
components.description: Top-of-sidebar progress bar toward next tier. Free-delivery badge at 8 items.

tech.notes: Tier thresholds: 4→5%, 5→8%, 7→11%, 8→Free Delivery, 9→14%, 11→17%, 13→20%. Documented in project_betterday_subscription_current.md.

components.name: Allergen + menu-change notice pills
components.description: Single-line clickable notices for allergen warnings and items swapped due to menu changes.

components.name: 3-wide swap grid
components.description: Centered modal showing same-category alternatives. 'Replace All' and 'Mix & Match' modes.

tech.notes: Demo in menu-overlay.html and swap-item.html. Auto-closes in Mix mode when slots filled.

modules.name: Pricing & Savings Tiers
modules.description: Server-authoritative price math so customer can't manipulate totals client-side.

components.name: Compute cart totals
components.description: Pure function: given cart items + config, return subtotal, tier discount %, delivery fee, tax estimate, total.

tech.notes: Pure function, fully testable. Reads tier thresholds from StoreSetting. Integer cents everywhere.

components.name: Free-delivery threshold logic
components.description: FREE_DELIVERY_MEALS = 8. Applies automatically when threshold met.

tech.notes: Configurable per store via StoreSetting.

modules.name: Cart Persistence
modules.description: Guest cart in localStorage, logged-in cart in DB, merge on login.

components.name: LocalStorage cart
components.description: Unauthenticated user can build a cart. Persists across page reloads.

tech.notes: Same shape as server cart, just persisted client-side.

components.name: Merge on login
components.description: When a guest logs in, merge their localStorage cart into their server cart (union of items, dedupe by meal_id).

categories.name: Checkout
categories.description: The path from 'I want to order' to 'order placed'. Express checkout, guest path, logged-in path, address, delivery window, coupons, tips, review, place.
categories.tech_notes: Stripe Elements for card entry. Stripe Payment Request API for Apple Pay / Google Pay. Own Checkout flow in Next.js — no redirect to Stripe Checkout (we want full brand control).

modules.name: Express Checkout
modules.description: The single biggest conversion lever for a D2C storefront. Above-the-fold one-tap buttons that skip most of the form.

components.name: Apple Pay merchant setup
components.description: Verify domain with Apple, register merchant ID, host verification file.

tech.notes: Stripe provides the verification file. Must be served at /.well-known/apple-developer-merchantid-domain-association.

components.name: Apple Pay button component
components.description: Above-stepper button. Launches Apple Pay sheet with cart total, captures address + payment in one tap.

tech.notes: Only rendered if browser supports Apple Pay.

components.name: Google Pay button component
components.description: Above-stepper button using Stripe Payment Request API (same primitive as Apple Pay).

components.name: Enable Stripe Link
components.description: Stripe's saved-payment-and-address system. Returning Link users can check out in a few clicks.

tech.notes: Enabled in Stripe Elements config. Free with Stripe.

components.name: Evaluate Shop Pay feasibility
components.description: Shop Pay is Shopify's express checkout. Requires a Shopify-adjacent integration path — may not be feasible without a Shopify storefront. Investigate before committing.

tech.notes: Open question: does this require Shop Pay Checkout Sheet Kit or a full Shopify storefront?

modules.name: Stepper flow
modules.description: Traditional multi-step form for customers who don't use express. Below the express row.

components.name: Address picker / new address form
components.description: If logged in, list saved addresses. Otherwise show 'enter new address' form. Validates against delivery zone.

components.name: Delivery zone validation
components.description: Check postal code against DeliveryZone table. Reject or offer pickup alternative.

components.name: Delivery date + window picker
components.description: Shows available delivery dates (respecting cutoff rules and holidays) and time windows per date.

components.name: Stripe Elements card entry
components.description: Embedded Stripe Elements for card entry. No PCI scope (Stripe handles it).

components.name: Saved payment method picker
components.description: Logged-in returning customers see saved cards first.

components.name: Order summary card
components.description: Final list of items, subtotal, discount, fees, tax, tip, total. Big 'Place Order' button.

components.name: Place order action
components.description: POST /api/orders — validates cart, charges Stripe, creates Order + OrderItems + Payment, returns order ID.

modules.name: Coupon + Gift Card at Checkout
modules.description: Apply coupon codes and gift card balances at checkout. Lives in cart summary area.

components.name: Validate coupon endpoint
components.description: POST /api/cart/apply-coupon — checks coupon rules against the current cart and customer (including order history).

components.name: Apply gift card code
components.description: Debit gift card balance against order total. Partial balance supported.

modules.name: Tip Selection
modules.description: Optional tip for the driver. Preset buttons + custom amount.

components.name: Tip selector component
components.description: Presets: 0%, 10%, 15%, 20%, custom. Updates totals live.

modules.name: Confirmation Page
modules.description: Post-place-order landing. Order summary, expected delivery, receipt email sent.

components.name: Confirmation UI
components.description: Big check mark, order number, summary, 'track your order' link, upsell to create account (guest path only).

components.name: Trigger receipt email
components.description: After successful order, fire a receipt email via Resend.

categories.name: Payments
categories.description: Stripe Elements for card entry. Stripe Subscriptions for recurring. Tax, tips, refunds, failed-payment retry, payment method management.
categories.tech_notes: All money as integer cents. Use Stripe Customer ID as our reference. Stripe Tax for CAN GST. Never store card data ourselves — Stripe holds everything.

modules.name: Stripe Core
modules.description: All payment processing runs through Stripe. Never store card data ourselves.

components.name: Stripe account + test mode
components.description: Create Stripe account, get test keys, configure business info for Canadian tax.

tech.notes: One-time manual setup. Save keys in password manager + env vars.

components.name: Webhook endpoint + signing secret
components.description: POST /api/webhooks/stripe with signature verification.

tech.notes: Stripe sends events for payment_intent.*, customer.subscription.*, etc.

components.name: Stripe customer creation
components.description: On first checkout, create a Stripe Customer and store stripe_customer_id on our Customer row.

components.name: Mount Elements in checkout
components.description: Embedded card input with 3DS support. No PCI scope for us.

components.name: Brand Elements styling
components.description: Match Stripe Elements theme to BetterDay brand.

tech.notes: Appearance API with cream/navy/sky palette.

components.name: Create PaymentIntent
components.description: POST /api/payments/intent — creates intent with amount + currency, returns client_secret.

components.name: Confirm + handle 3DS
components.description: Frontend calls stripe.confirmPayment; handle challenge flow.

components.name: Idempotency keys
components.description: Every charge creation uses an idempotency key tied to the order so retries don't double-charge.

components.name: Dynamic price objects
components.description: Since build-a-cart changes amount weekly, use dynamic pricing via invoice items instead of fixed Prices.

tech.notes: Alternative: create Price at Friday rollover. TBD which is cleaner.

components.name: Create subscription
components.description: POST subscription with a Customer, a default payment method, and a billing cycle anchor.

components.name: Update subscription (next week)
components.description: When cart changes for next week, update the upcoming invoice to reflect new total.

components.name: Cancel subscription
components.description: Immediate or end-of-period cancel.

modules.name: Saved Payment Methods
modules.description: Let returning customers check out with one tap.

components.name: SetupIntent for off-session future use
components.description: Saves card for subscription billing without a charge.

components.name: Default payment method
components.description: Customer can set which card is default for subscriptions + future orders.

components.name: GET /api/account/payment-methods
components.description: Return saved cards with last4, brand, expiry, default flag.

components.name: DELETE /api/account/payment-methods/:id
components.description: Detach payment method from Stripe customer.

components.name: Saved cards list (account hub)
components.description: Card rows showing brand + last4 + expiry + default badge.

components.name: Add new card flow
components.description: Stripe Elements modal to add a card, uses SetupIntent.

modules.name: Tax
modules.description: Canadian GST handling via Stripe Tax.

components.name: Enable Stripe Tax for Canada
components.description: Register BetterDay for GST, enable Stripe Tax in dashboard, set product tax codes.

components.name: Calculate tax on intent creation
components.description: Include customer address; Stripe returns tax amount which we add to order total.

components.name: Line item on receipts
components.description: Always show GST as a separate line on checkout + receipt.

modules.name: Refunds & Credits
modules.description: Issue refunds or in-app credit for problems.

components.name: POST /api/admin/orders/:id/refund
components.description: Full refund via Stripe; updates Order status to 'refunded'.

components.name: POST /api/admin/orders/:id/refund with amount
components.description: Refund a specific amount, log reason, keep order active.

components.name: Issue store credit
components.description: Credit to customer's StoreCredit balance instead of card refund.

components.name: Apply credit at checkout
components.description: Customer opts in to use credit balance at checkout; debited at order placement.

modules.name: Payment Failures & Dunning
modules.description: Handling failed charges gracefully.

components.name: payment_intent.payment_failed webhook handler
components.description: On failure, mark Payment as failed, notify customer, schedule retry.

components.name: Smart retry schedule
components.description: Retry at 1d, 3d, 7d with exponential backoff. After final fail, pause subscription.

components.name: Failed payment email
components.description: Send 'card on file declined' email with update-card CTA.

components.name: Failed payment SMS
components.description: Optional SMS for urgent cases (right before cutoff).

components.name: Persistent 'update your card' banner
components.description: Shown at top of account hub when a payment method needs attention.

categories.name: Orders (One-Time)
categories.description: Creating, viewing, tracking, and reordering one-time orders. History, detail, status updates.
categories.tech_notes: Order and OrderItem tables. Status lifecycle: pending → confirmed → in_production → out_for_delivery → delivered. Snapshots meal data at order time.

categories.name: Subscriptions
categories.description: Weekly subscription engine. Friday rollover creates next week's WeeklyCartRecord. Thursday cutoff locks and charges. Auto dish-swap on menu changes.
categories.tech_notes: Designed in project_betterday_skip_pause_model.md. Unified cart report query. Single delivery_status enum. No placeholder leakage (fixes the Sprwt problem).

categories.name: Plan Management
categories.description: Skip, pause, cancel, and related lifecycle actions on an active subscription. Retention gauntlet on cancel.
categories.tech_notes: Unified skip/pause/cancel model already designed. Skip is universal week-level action; pause = indefinite only; cancel = terminal. Demo exists in subscriber-hub.html.

categories.name: Delivery & Pickup
categories.description: Delivery zones, pickup locations, scheduling, delivery windows, holidays, route hints for fulfillment.
categories.tech_notes: DeliveryZone, PickupLocation, Holiday, CutoffRule tables. Calgary zones only at launch.

categories.name: Coupons & Discounts
categories.description: The coupon engine HMP can't ship. Full restriction set: first-order, after-N-orders, category, zone, email allow/deny, stacking rules.
categories.tech_notes: Coupon, CouponRedemption tables. CustomerMirror.orders_completed_count for live-verify at redemption.

categories.name: Gift Cards
categories.description: Purchase, schedule delivery, redeem at checkout. Balance tracking, partial redemption.
categories.tech_notes: GiftCard, GiftCardTransaction tables. Stripe for purchase. Resend for delivery email.

categories.name: Rewards & Loyalty
categories.description: Points per order, redemption for discounts or free items, tier system, points history.
categories.tech_notes: RewardAccount, RewardTransaction, RewardRule tables. Earning rules as config, not code.

categories.name: Referrals
categories.description: Share link, track referrer → referee, both sides get coupons on successful conversion.
categories.tech_notes: Referral, ReferralReward tables. Coupon engine handles the reward codes.

categories.name: Notifications
categories.description: Transactional email (receipts, password reset, delivery updates), SMS delivery alerts, push notifications, in-app toasts.
categories.tech_notes: Resend for email. Twilio (non-Verify) for SMS delivery updates. FCM for web push (later).

categories.name: Admin Console
categories.description: Staff surface. Order management, customer management, menu publishing, coupon management, content, impersonation, support tools.
categories.tech_notes: Can share the same codebase as the customer app, gated by role. Or separate Next.js subapp under /admin.

categories.name: Analytics & Reports
categories.description: Revenue dashboards, cohort analytics, product/category performance, cart report query for kitchen production.
categories.tech_notes: Probably Metabase or a simple built-in dashboard for launch. Real BI can come later.

categories.name: Integrations
categories.description: Stripe, Twilio Verify, Resend, Google/Facebook OAuth, culinary-ops bridge, HMP one-shot migration, POS (future), delivery routing (future).
categories.tech_notes: Wrap every external dependency in a thin adapter so we can swap vendors.

categories.name: Platform & Ops
categories.description: The invisible stuff: logging, monitoring, cron runner, job queue, error tracking, secrets management, deployments, backups.
categories.tech_notes: Railway or Vercel for hosting. Inngest or pg_cron for jobs. Sentry for errors. Env vars from a vault.

modules.name: Hosting & Deploy
modules.description: Where the webapp and API actually run.

components.name: Vercel setup for webapp
components.description: Deploy betterday-webapp Next.js app to Vercel. Connect to GitHub for auto-deploy on main.

components.name: Environment variables config
components.description: Stripe keys, Twilio, Resend, DB URL, JWT secret, OAuth client IDs, etc.

components.name: Preview deployments for PRs
components.description: Every PR gets a preview URL. Useful for design review.

components.name: Railway or Render for NestJS API
components.description: Host the NestJS backend. Consider: Railway (cheaper, simpler) vs Render (current culinary-ops).

tech.notes: Decision pending. Render already has culinary-ops, reusing keeps one dashboard.

components.name: North America region
components.description: Ensure API runs in Ohio/Oregon, not Singapore. Culinary-ops currently Singapore (fix separately).

components.name: Neon Postgres
components.description: Shared DB with culinary-ops. One database, multiple schemas or one schema with clear table prefixes.

components.name: Neon branching for preview
components.description: Each PR preview gets a DB branch so migrations can be tested in isolation.

components.name: Connection pooling
components.description: Use Neon pooler or PgBouncer for serverless-friendly connection reuse.

components.name: Cloudflare R2 or AWS S3
components.description: For meal photos, user uploads, generated receipts. Move off culinary-ops local disk.

tech.notes: R2 has no egress fees, cheaper than S3 for a read-heavy image workload.

components.name: CDN in front of images
components.description: Cloudflare or Vercel image optimization for menu photos.

modules.name: CI/CD
modules.description: Automated testing and deployment pipelines.

components.name: Lint + typecheck
components.description: Run on every PR: eslint, tsc --noEmit, prettier --check.

components.name: Unit + integration tests
components.description: vitest or jest runs against a test Postgres.

components.name: Prisma validate + migrate check
components.description: Fail PR if migrations are missing or schema is invalid.

components.name: Build check
components.description: Run next build + nest build on PRs to catch compile errors.

components.name: Main branch protection rules
components.description: Require PR review, passing CI, linear history on main.

modules.name: Observability
modules.description: Logs, errors, metrics, so we can see what's happening in production.

components.name: Sentry for web + API
components.description: Capture frontend errors + backend exceptions + performance traces.

components.name: Upload source maps
components.description: CI uploads source maps to Sentry for readable stack traces.

components.name: Structured logging setup
components.description: JSON logs via pino or winston, shipped to a central log sink.

components.name: Request ID propagation
components.description: Every API request gets an ID that's threaded through logs + passed to frontend for support.

components.name: Uptime monitoring
components.description: External ping service (Better Uptime or similar) for public endpoints.

components.name: Business metrics dashboard
components.description: Orders/hour, MRR, churn. Feeds into analytics category.

tech.notes: Likely Metabase against the prod DB.

modules.name: Secrets Management
modules.description: Where API keys, tokens, and other sensitive values live.

components.name: Vercel env vars
components.description: Production secrets in Vercel encrypted env vars. Separate per environment.

components.name: Local .env.local
components.description: Dev secrets in .env.local, gitignored.

tech.notes: Ship .env.example with placeholders.

components.name: Rotation procedure
components.description: Documented process for rotating each secret when needed.

tech.notes: Write runbook in /docs/ops/secret-rotation.md

modules.name: Background Jobs
modules.description: Cron + queue runner for scheduled work (Friday rollover, Thursday cutoff, etc.).

components.name: Runner decision (pg_cron vs Inngest vs BullMQ)
components.description: Pick one. Inngest is serverless-friendly, pg_cron is simplest, BullMQ needs Redis.

tech.notes: Lean Inngest for cleanliness, pg_cron for zero-infra start.

components.name: Runner setup
components.description: Install chosen runner, wire into NestJS modules.

components.name: Friday rollover cron
components.description: Every Friday morning, create next week's WeeklyCartRecord for active subscribers.

components.name: Thursday cutoff cron
components.description: Every Thursday at 23:59, lock 'scheduled' carts into 'confirmed' and charge.

components.name: Payment retry scheduler
components.description: Daily scan for failed payments needing retry per dunning schedule.

components.name: Weekly menu advance
components.description: Flip next week's menu live on Sunday evening.

components.name: Old session cleanup
components.description: Daily sweep of expired Session, OtpCode rows.

modules.name: Security Hardening
modules.description: Baseline security controls.

components.name: Content-Security-Policy
components.description: Strict CSP allowing Stripe, Twilio, Resend, own origins.

components.name: HSTS
components.description: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload.

components.name: X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, etc.
components.description: Standard hardening headers.

components.name: CORS policy
components.description: Allow only storefront domain + admin domain. Reject everything else.

components.name: Global rate limits
components.description: Per-IP and per-identity limits on sensitive endpoints.

components.name: AuditLog model
components.description: Table: id, actor_id, action, target_type, target_id, metadata, created_at.

components.name: Audit log writer service
components.description: NestJS service that every mutation calls to log the action.

modules.name: Backups & DR
modules.description: Recovery story if something goes wrong.

components.name: Neon automatic backups
components.description: Neon has point-in-time restore built in. Verify retention window.

components.name: Weekly full export to cold storage
components.description: Cron that dumps to R2 for independent off-platform backup.

components.name: DR runbook
components.description: Step-by-step recovery playbook: DB loss, Vercel outage, Stripe issue, etc.

tech.notes: /docs/ops/runbooks/

categories.name: Corporate B2B
categories.description: Gurleen's existing corporate ordering module stays separate for now. A 2.0 integration with the D2C storefront is a future project.
categories.tech_notes: Already exists in culinary-ops: CorporateCompany, CorporateEmployee, CorporateOrder, CorporateOrderItem, CorporateBenefitLevel, CorporateParLevel, CorporateCompanyPIN, CorporateMagicToken, CompanyInvoice, CorporateCreditNote, CorporateMonthlyStatement, CorporateSetting. Magic-link auth, par-level inventory, monthly statements. Do not touch without coordination.
